2010-04-01.title: Zimbabwe: New Charge in Terror Trial
2010-04-01.abstract: Roy Bennett, one of Prime Minister Morgan Tsvangirai’s closest allies, will face a new charge of hoarding maize nine years ago.

2010-04-01.title: France: More Publishers Sue Google
2010-04-01.abstract: Several French publishing houses intend to bring a joint copyright infringement suit against Google, publishing executives said this week.

2010-04-01.title: Belgium: Burqa Bill in Parliament
2010-04-01.abstract: A committee of Belgian lawmakers voted Wednesday to ban the wearing of burqas  in public, paving the way for the first clampdown of its kind on the Continent.

2010-04-01.title: The Iraq Drawdown and Afghan Buildup
2010-04-01.abstract: In one of the most complex logistical operations in military history, American troops and equipment are being pulled out of  Iraq and sent on to Afghanistan, or back home to refit the armed services.

2010-04-01.title: No Shortcuts When Military Moves a War
2010-04-01.abstract: The movement of U.S. forces from Iraq to Afghanistan is one of the largest such operations since World War II.

2010-04-02.title: Top European Clerics Defend Benedict
2010-04-02.abstract: Several prominent European churchmen on Thursday argued that Pope Benedict XVI should not be criticized for his oversight of cases of priestly sexual abuse.

2010-04-02.title: Despite Few Hunters, Seal Pups Face Threats
2010-04-02.abstract: The same warm weather that has kept hunters’ boats in harbors has also reduced the amount of ice, a main habitat for harp seals.

2010-04-02.title: Kerry Tries to Nudge Syria to Re-engage With the U.S.
2010-04-02.abstract: The meeting is an indication that the United States is moving to re-engage with Syria as the Obama administration works to resuscitate Middle East peace talks.

2010-04-02.title: How a Molesting Case Emerged Decades Later
2010-04-02.abstract: An accuser of a pedophile priest contacted the church, and the priest himself, after seeing photographs on the Internet of him still working with children.

2010-04-02.title: Drought Along the Mekong River
2010-04-02.abstract: A severe drought has dried up farmers’ fields and left tens of millions of people short of water in southern China and Southeast Asia.

2010-04-03.title: From California to Cambodia, Fighting for Women
2010-04-03.abstract: The daughter of a well-to-do merchant in Phnom Penh who had been sent to the West at the age of 18 is now in Cambodia’s Parliament.

2010-04-03.title: 3 Germans Die in Afghan Clash
2010-04-03.abstract: Three German soldiers were killed and five were wounded in a firefight with insurgents near the city of Kunduz in northern Afghanistan.

2010-04-03.title: Pakistan: A Raft of Amendments
2010-04-03.abstract: The Pakistani government introduced a package of constitutional changes in Parliament that would shift the balance of power from the president to the prime minister.

2010-04-03.title: A Long Chat for Obama and Hu
2010-04-03.abstract: President Obama spoke by phone with President Hu Jintao of China for about an hour — so long that Air Force One had to be held on the tarmac at Andrews Air Force Base after landing.

2010-04-03.title: 6 Afghans Die as Their Unit Is Hit in Error by Germans
2010-04-03.abstract: A contingent of Afghan reinforcements delivering supplies to a German military base was mistakenly fired on by German troops, and six Afghan soldiers were killed.

2010-04-04.title: Corrections
2010-04-04.abstract: A news analysis article last Sunday about coming elections in Britain, where the opposition Conservatives hope to end the Labour Party’s 13 years in power, misstated the age of Labour’s leader, Prime Minister Gordon Brown. He is 59, not 57.

2010-04-04.title: White Supremacist Is Killed in South Africa
2010-04-04.abstract: A man and a teenage boy have been charged with the killing of Eugene TerreBlanche, who served a sentence for beating a black worker and setting dogs on a gas station attendant.

2010-04-04.title: Bombs Hit Hub of Diplomacy in Baghdad
2010-04-04.abstract: The attacks, at a delicate moment in talks to form a new government, were an attempt by insurgents to retake the initiative after years of retreat.

2010-04-04.title: Finally, Vettel Wins a Race
2010-04-04.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR — After scoring pole position at the first two races and dropping from the lead with a technical problem each time, Sebastian Vettel leaped from third on the grid at the Malaysian Grand Prix on Sunday.

2010-04-04.title: Protesters Turn Up Heat in Thailand
2010-04-04.abstract: Demonstrators defied calls to disperse in a major escalation of three weeks of mass protests.

2010-04-05.title: U.S. Admits Role in February Killing of Afghan Women
2010-04-05.abstract: The United States reversed course on its account of a botched Special Operations raid after an investigative report said there were signs of evidence tampering.

2010-04-05.title: Colonial-Era Dispute Agitates South Koreans
2010-04-05.abstract: South Koreans still ask whether they should expose pro-Japanese collaborators, most of them already dead.

2010-04-05.title: Mentally Disturbed Man Attacks Bishop in Germany
2010-04-05.abstract: The bishop was unharmed and there appeared to be no connection to the unfolding church scandal.

2010-04-05.title: Drones Batter Al Qaeda and Its Allies Within Pakistan
2010-04-05.abstract: A stepped-up campaign of American drone strikes has reportedly cast a pall of fear over an area that was once a free zone for Al Qaeda and the Taliban.

2010-04-05.title: Spring Harvest of Debt for Parched Farms in Southern China
2010-04-05.abstract: The soil on roughly 30,000 square miles of farmland is too dry to plant crops, with agricultural losses already totaling $3.5 billion.

2010-04-06.title: Visual Diary: Shooting by Car in Kandahar
2010-04-06.abstract: Christoph Bangert shares photographs from his recent reporting trip to Kandahar, perhaps some of the last photos to be taken by a Western photographer in the city, which is bracing for a major offensive in the coming months.

2010-04-06.title: Video Shows U.S. Killing of Reuters Employees
2010-04-06.abstract: Wikileaks.org released a video of a U.S. helicopter killing  a Reuters photographer and driver in 2007 in Baghdad.

2010-04-06.title: Obama Limits When U.S. Would Use Nuclear Arms
2010-04-06.abstract: President Obama described his policy as part of a broader effort to edge the world toward making nuclear weapons obsolete.

2010-04-06.title: Group Releases Classified Video of 2007 Baghdad Attack
2010-04-06.abstract: A nonprofit whistleblowing group has released classified military video of a 2007 attack in Baghdad that left a dozen civilians, including two journalists, dead.

2010-04-06.title: Ayatollah Supports Bid to Sharply Cut Iran Subsidies
2010-04-06.abstract: Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, offered a crucial gesture of public support for President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad’s effort to enact a sweeping economic reform plan.

2010-04-07.title: Palestinians Try a Less Violent Path to Resistance
2010-04-07.abstract: The Palestinian Authority is trying to build a virtual state and body politic through nonviolent resistance.

2010-04-07.title: Iraq Video Brings Notice to a Web Site
2010-04-07.abstract: With the release of a video of a U.S. assault on civilians, WikiLeaks edged closer toward a form of journalism.

2010-04-07.title: Abuse Crisis Strains Vatican’s Ancient Ways of Management
2010-04-07.abstract: That Pope Benedict XVI’s papacy has gone from crisis to crisis points to its difficulties as an institution still struggling with modernity.

2010-04-07.title: U.S. Approves Targeted Killing of American Cleric
2010-04-07.abstract: The Obama administration authorized the killing of the radical Muslim cleric Anwar al-Awlaki.

2010-04-07.title: Obama’s Nuclear Strategy Intended as a Message
2010-04-07.abstract: The strategy makes just about every nonnuclear state immune from any threat of nuclear retaliation by the United States, but it carves out an exception for Iran and North Korea.

2010-04-08.title: In a Tour for Visitors, Baghdad’s Past Is Present
2010-04-08.abstract: Accompanied by heavy security, a group of foreigners recently visited historic sites that most foreigners have not had access to for decades and that Iraqis are often too frightened to visit.

2010-04-08.title: Psychologists Explain Iraq Airstrike Video
2010-04-08.abstract: Seemingly cruel chatter in a helicopter may reflect a wartime necessity to overcome inhibitions to kill.

2010-04-08.title: China’s Censors Tackle and Trip Over the Internet
2010-04-08.abstract: The Communist Party’s censorship machine is highly sophisticated in some ways, remarkably crude in others.

2010-04-08.title: U.S. Tries to Mend Rift With Karzai
2010-04-08.abstract: After a week of heated words, a State Department official said Afghanistan’s president, Hamid Karzai, is “a figure that we respect.”

2010-04-08.title: Rwanda: 6 Drown on Their Way to Genocide Memorial
2010-04-08.abstract: At least 10 people were still missing, and more than 50 rescued from the waters, after their boat capsized Wednesday in heavy winds on Lake Kivu off the coast.

2010-04-09.title: Rumor of Infidelities Sets Off Modern French Farce
2010-04-09.abstract: A Twitter posting has evolved into a psychodrama, with President Nicolas Sarkozy suspecting a plot against him.

2010-04-09.title: Netanyahu Cancels Trip to U.S. Nuclear Summit
2010-04-09.abstract: Israeli media reported that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu feared that Muslim states were planning on using the occasion to discuss Israel’s nuclear arsenal.

2010-04-09.title: South Africa: World Bank to Finance Coal-Fired Plant
2010-04-09.abstract: Eskom, the government-controlled utility, got its $3.75 billion loan on Thursday from the World Bank, mostly to finance a giant coal-fired power plant.

2010-04-09.title: Crowd Mourns Victims in Kyrgyzstan
2010-04-09.abstract: Bloodstains still visible on the pavement, crowds gathered for a memorial and demanded that the president resign.

2010-04-09.title: NATO Aircraft Crashes in Afghanistan
2010-04-09.abstract: The crash killed three American service members and one civilian employee, according to NATO officials.

2010-04-10.title: Challenging Iran, With YouTube Views
2010-04-10.abstract: Mohsen Sazegara, a figure in the Islamic Revolution, is taking on the system he helped create from his basement in Virginia.

2010-04-10.title: Pakistan Begins to Ease Backlog of Visas for American Diplomats
2010-04-10.abstract: At the same time, the United States is working on increasing payments to Pakistan for its efforts against terrorism.

2010-04-10.title: Canada: Honors at an Era’s End
2010-04-10.abstract: Canada marked the death of its last living World War I veteran with solemn ceremonies at the National War Memorial in Ottawa and at a Canadian memorial near Vimy, France.

2010-04-10.title: Sri Lanka: Governing Party Wins Large Majority in Vote
2010-04-10.abstract: The governing party won a large majority in parliamentary elections, cementing President Mahinda Rajapaksa’s control over a nation struggling to recover from civil war.

2010-04-10.title: Pope Put Off Punishing Abusive Priest
2010-04-10.abstract: Cardinal Joseph Ratzinger, then a Vatican official, signed a letter in 1985 resisting defrocking a priest who abused children, according to church documents.

2010-04-11.title: Poland Feels Shock at the Size of Its Loss
2010-04-11.abstract: With no answers as to why the country had been robbed of many of its brightest minds, Poles found reassurance in the presence of so many others in the same state of shock.

2010-04-11.title: Small Bomb Is Thrown at U.S. Post in Mexico
2010-04-11.abstract: Windows were shattered but no one was injured when the device went off at the United States Consulate in the border city of Nuevo Laredo.

2010-04-11.title: Amid Uncertainty, Poland Shows Political Resilience
2010-04-11.abstract: The electoral mechanisms to replace the dead officials were functioning according to plan, and senior Polish officials said the government would continue to operate normally.

2010-04-11.title: After Clashes, Thai Standoff Deepens
2010-04-11.abstract: Thailand’s crisis is bound to continue, the product of a tectonic shift in which the country’s poor majority has begun to challenge the hierarchy of power.

2010-04-11.title: Poland in Mourning After Crash
2010-04-11.abstract: The body of Poland's president was returned to Warsaw a day after he and dozens of top Polish political and military leaders died in a plane crash in Russia.

2010-04-12.title: Artists Embellish Walls With Political Visions
2010-04-12.abstract: The government of Venezuela is making street art a central element of its promotion of a state ideology.

2010-04-12.title: Polish Crash Inquiry Looks at Decision to Land Jet
2010-04-12.abstract: In 2008, the Polish president, Lech Kaczynski, reportedly demanded that a pilot land despite unsafe conditions.

2010-04-12.title: Obama Meets With a Parade of Leaders
2010-04-12.abstract: President Obama prepared for the opening of his nuclear security summit meeting by warning that Al Qaeda was still seeking materials to build an atomic bomb.

2010-04-12.title: Leaders Gather for Nuclear Talks as New Threat Is Seen
2010-04-12.abstract: A nuclear arms race may be escalating in South Asia, but the problem is not set to be discussed when President Obama’s nuclear security summit meeting opens Monday in Washington.

2010-04-12.title: Fuel Sales to U.S. at Issue in Kyrgyzstan
2010-04-12.abstract: Leaders in the interim government accused the United States of allowing family of the ousted president to enrich themselves with Pentagon contracts.

2010-04-13.title: Bootlegging, Pakistan-Style
2010-04-13.abstract: Purveyors of contraband alcohol must hide their line of work from the police, the Taliban and their families.

2010-04-13.title: Vatican Publishes Procedures for Sex Abuse Cases
2010-04-13.abstract: Fending off accusations that it obstructed justice, the Vatican put a guide online urging bishops to report abuse to civil authorities if required by local law.

2010-04-13.title: French and German Ties Fray Over Greek Crisis
2010-04-13.abstract: Germany, long the financier of the European Union, has made it clear that it will no longer pay for the mistakes and frauds of others, while France has emphasized unity.

2010-04-13.title: Kazakhstan to Permit Military Overflights to Afghanistan
2010-04-13.abstract: The deal opens a direct and faster route over the North Pole for American forces and lethal equipment headed to Afghanistan.

2010-04-13.title: Political Graffiti
2010-04-13.abstract: Government-financed brigades of graffiti artists and muralists are blanketing the walls of Caracas, Venezuela, with politicized images, ranging from crude, semi-anonymous graffiti tags to bold, colorful works.

2010-04-14.title: Mideast Land Conflict Now Includes Street Signs
2010-04-14.abstract: A dispute over naming streets in Ramallah is an example of how Israelis and Palestinians are almost incapable of hearing one another.

2010-04-14.title: British Election Issues: Frugality and Afghanistan
2010-04-14.abstract: The economy is a prominent concern in the race, but the loss of British troops in Afghanistan could tip the balance.

2010-04-14.title: Poland’s Unity Is Disrupted by Plans for President’s Interment
2010-04-14.abstract: Opposition is building toward plans to inter President Lech Kaczynski and his wife, Maria, in Wawel Cathedral in Krakow, a resting place for Polish kings and other notables.

2010-04-14.title: Obama Puts His Own Mark on Foreign Policy Issues
2010-04-14.abstract: Obama’s realpolitik approach is focused more on relations with world powers than on human rights and democracy.

2010-04-14.title: Iraq’s Forces Prove Able, but Loyalty Is Uncertain
2010-04-14.abstract: Security forces kept Iraq safe for voting, but there is no new government and no certain leader they can look to.

2010-04-15.title: Sudan’s Growth Buoys a Leader Reviled Elsewhere
2010-04-15.abstract: Many Sudanese attribute their country’s progress to President Omar Hassan al-Bashir, a suspected war criminal often perceived as a villain in the West.

2010-04-15.title: Rain Multiplies Woes of Rio de Janeiro’s Squatters
2010-04-15.abstract: Last week’s deadly storm has raised questions about the Olympic city’s emergency readiness and revived debate over squatter communities.

2010-04-15.title: Obama Speech Signals a U.S. Shift on Middle East
2010-04-15.abstract: A statement that the conflict is vital to U.S. security showed how hard President Obama might push for peace.

2010-04-15.title: Indonesia: Protests After Rumor That Tomb Would Be Moved
2010-04-15.abstract: At least two people were killed when hundreds of protesters clashed with riot police in Jakarta after rumors spread that the city planned to remove the tomb of a Muslim cleric from the area.

2010-04-15.title: Congo: Rapes by Civilians Rise Sharply, Study Says
2010-04-15.abstract: A new study shows the total number of sexual assault cases decreased between 2004 and 2008. But the number of rapes committed by civilians increased 17-fold.

2010-04-16.title: Attacks on Military Computers Cited
2010-04-16.abstract: Lt. Gen. Keith B. Alexander, nominated to head a new Cyber Command, said the military systems underwent attacks thousands of times a day.

2010-04-16.title: U.N. Report Finds Faults in Pakistani Bhutto Inquiry
2010-04-16.abstract: An investigation into the assassination of Benazir Bhutto concluded that the failure of Pakistani authorities to effectively investigate the killing was “deliberate.”

2010-04-16.title: In a First, 2 British Leaders Vying to Be the Premier Debate, but a Third Face Shines
2010-04-16.abstract: The debate between the men contending to be prime minister offered few parallels to the moments that have swung American elections: no obvious gaffes, no memorable one-liners, little by way of humor.

2010-04-16.title: Air Travel Chaos Deepens Into Weekend
2010-04-16.abstract: Travel chaos around the world, caused by a volcano in Iceland, grew with little reprieve seen for the weekend.

2010-04-16.title: U.S. Nuclear Threat
2010-04-16.abstract: Conventional wisdom is that the Cold War danger nuclear missile strikes has given way to a new threat: nuclear devices in the hands of terrorists.

2010-04-17.title: Iraqi Sunnis Expect Allegiance Shift to Bear Fruit
2010-04-17.abstract: Former Prime Minister Ayad Allawi has managed to convince Sunnis that he can end sectarianism and protect their rights.

2010-04-17.title: 10 Years After a Mea Culpa, No Hint of a ‘Me, Too’
2010-04-17.abstract: As’ad Shaftari, a former intelligence officer in a feared Christian militia, published an apology for his war crimes near the April anniversary of the beginning of the Lebanese civil war.

2010-04-17.title: Voters’ Concerns on Immigration Spin British Campaign
2010-04-17.abstract: In a blue-collar borough where old and new collide, the ultra-right-wing British Nationalist Party finds a wedge.

2010-04-17.title: Amazon Dam Project Pits Economic Benefit Against Protection of Indigenous Lands
2010-04-17.abstract: A plan to build the third largest dam in the world is highlighting Brazil’s struggle to balance growth and protect its ecosystems and indigenous communities.

2010-04-17.title: China Starts New Bureau to Curb Web
2010-04-17.abstract: The authorities are said to be retooling their media apparatus to deepen their leverage over the Web, and regulators are jostling for the growing power and prestige at stake.

2010-04-18.title: Gates Says U.S. Lacks a Policy to Thwart Iran
2010-04-18.abstract: A secret memo set off a search for new options in dealing with Iran’s steady progress toward nuclear capability.

2010-04-18.title: Michel Chartrand, Fiery Labor Union Leader in Quebec, Is Dead at 93
2010-04-18.abstract: Mr. Chartrand was a firebrand whose support of leftist politics and Quebec nationalism landed him in jail and buoyed a radical separatist group.

2010-04-18.title: Airlines Press Europe to Ease Ban on Flights
2010-04-18.abstract: As Europe grounded most airline flights for a fourth day, desperate airlines ran test flights and pressed aviation authorities to loosen the flight ban.

2010-04-18.title: Button Beats the Elements and Takes Victory in Chinese Grand Prix
2010-04-18.abstract: Jenson Button drove a brilliant race through the rain, making a good strategic choice with tires, to win his second race of the season at McLaren Mercedes.

2010-04-18.title: Poland Mourns, but Some Look Ahead to Elections
2010-04-18.abstract: Some 150,000 Poles bade farewell to their president in a funeral that capped more than a week of mourning.

2010-04-19.title: Indian Who Built Yoga Empire Works on Politics
2010-04-19.abstract: Swami Ramdev, a yogic fusion of Richard Simmons, Dr. Oz and Oprah Winfrey, said he would establish a political party.

2010-04-19.title: Before Kyrgyz Uprising, Dose of Russian Soft Power
2010-04-19.abstract: Russia’s stance in Kyrgyzstan shows how Moscow approaches the former Soviet republics it regards as within its sphere of influence.

2010-04-19.title: Gates Pushes Back on Report of Memo About Iran Policy
2010-04-19.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates acknowledged writing the memo but said his goal was “to contribute to an orderly and timely decision-making process.”

2010-04-19.title: In Iceland, Mostly Clear Consciences
2010-04-19.abstract: While they are careful not to appear to take pleasure in this latest bout of troubles, Icelanders have met the volcanic eruption mostly with a collective sigh of relief.

2010-04-19.title: Corrections
2010-04-19.abstract: An article on Friday about a Russian-sponsored conference on computer security misidentified the United States agency for which Stewart A. Baker, a panelist at the conference, once served as chief counsel. Is the National Security Agency, not the National Security Council.

2010-04-20.title: Debate on Internet’s Limits Grows in Indonesia
2010-04-20.abstract: Social networking has become a force testing the boundaries of free expression, government regulation and the power of news media.

2010-04-20.title: Europe to Ease Aviation Ban, Amid Criticism
2010-04-20.abstract: A plan could begin to unravel the air travel disruption, but critics say the European Union has failed a test of leadership.

2010-04-20.title: Mexico’s First Lady Quietly Takes Strong Stance
2010-04-20.abstract: Margarita Zavala, the wife of President Felipe Calderón, has become, according to one poll, the most popular figure in her husband’s party.

2010-04-20.title: Insurers See Millions in Claims by Travelers
2010-04-20.abstract: Travel insurance companies in the United States say they are typically paying stranded travelers up to $250 a day as they wait for clear skies to return from Europe.

2010-04-20.title: When It Comes to Volcanic Ash, Airline Procedure Is Clear: Avoid
2010-04-20.abstract: Even though volcanoes have always been a threat to aviation, there are no guidelines for dealing with volcanic ash clouds other than to avoid them.

2010-04-21.title: Dispute Flares After NATO Convoy Kills 4 in Afghanistan
2010-04-21.abstract: Afghan officials in Khost say a NATO military convoy shot and killed four civilians, but NATO says the victims were two insurgents and their “associates.”

2010-04-21.title: China: Street Crime Sweep in Shanghai
2010-04-21.abstract: As part of a crackdown ahead of the coming World Expo in Shanghai, police officers have detained about 6,000 people in the city suspected of street crimes like theft and prostitution.

2010-04-21.title: Somalia: Off Again, on Again Radio
2010-04-21.abstract: Two radio stations in Somalia were shut down by the transitional government because they had stopped broadcasting music after threats from Islamist insurgents, but the stations were allowed back on the air hours later.

2010-04-21.title: In Pakistan, Violence on Campus
2010-04-21.abstract: At Pakistan's largest public university, the beating of a professor by members of an Islamic student group highlights a power struggle over the future of the country.

2010-04-21.title: Cool Debater Helps Britain’s Also-Ran Party
2010-04-21.abstract: In the wake of Parliament scandals, the leader of the Liberal Democrats, Nick Clegg, has enough support to change an election between the major parties.

2010-04-22.title: Trying to Save Wild Tigers by Rehabilitating Them
2010-04-22.abstract: Animal experts are experimenting with trying to rehabilitate and release into the wild tigers that have attacked and killed people.

2010-04-22.title: Japan Tries to Face Up to Growing Poverty Problem
2010-04-22.abstract: After years of economic stagnation and widening income disparities, this once proudly egalitarian nation is facing the fact that it has a growing number of poor people.

2010-04-22.title: At Least 6 People Abducted in Mexican Hotel Raids
2010-04-22.abstract: Gunmen hauled guests and employees from two hotels in Monterrey, causing a wave of panic.

2010-04-22.title: Ash Turns Routine Flights Into Overland Odysseys
2010-04-22.abstract: Airports rumbled back to life across Europe, but travelers who opted for land or sea journeys told of epic adventures.

2010-04-22.title: Japan Bans Beef Exports
2010-04-22.abstract: Japanese authorities imposed a temporary ban on beef exports after finding suspected cases of foot-and-mouth disease in cows on a single farm in southern Japan.

2010-04-23.title: Chilean Abuse Case Tests Loyalty of a Parish
2010-04-23.abstract: Chilean bishops face the prospect of weighing their loyalty to a revered priest against expectations that they handle abuse cases with greater transparency.

2010-04-23.title: Round 2 in Britain: Old Guard Rebounds
2010-04-23.abstract: Nick Clegg, the Liberal Democratic candidate, was strongly challenged by Prime Minister Gordon Brown and David Cameron of the Conservatives.

2010-04-23.title: Iraq: Navy Seals Member Cleared
2010-04-23.abstract: A member of the Navy Seals was cleared Thursday of charges that he covered up the beating of an Iraqi prisoner suspected of leading the 2004 killings of four American security contractors.

2010-04-23.title: Pakistan: More Rest, More Energy
2010-04-23.abstract: The government announced national power-saving austerity measures on Thursday in an effort to ease the country’s deepening electricity crisis

2010-04-23.title: Germany: Merkel Says Troops Still Needed in Afghanistan
2010-04-23.abstract: Faced with public opposition, Chancellor Angela Merkel told legislators Thursday that German troops were still needed there to prevent the spread of international terrorism.

2010-04-24.title: A Top Indonesian Lawyer May Be Honest to a Fault
2010-04-24.abstract: In a country where bribes play an integral part in the legal system, Hotman Paris Hutapea says that he would be a hypocrite to say he or other lawyers are “clean.”

2010-04-24.title: Companies Feeling More Pressure to Cut Iran Ties
2010-04-24.abstract: While the U.S. struggles to negotiate stronger sanctions on Iran, companies are being pushed to sever ties.

2010-04-24.title: Rebellious Mood Takes Root in Rural Thailand
2010-04-24.abstract: A rural region forms much of the core of the red shirt movement, demonstrating the magnitude of the challenge facing the government.

2010-04-24.title: Kyrgyzstan: A Promise to Stay Away
2010-04-24.abstract: Kyrgyzstan’s ousted president said Friday that he would not try to return to the country, but he maintained that his resignation was not valid.

2010-04-24.title: Zimbabwe: Iranian President Opens Trade Fair
2010-04-24.abstract: President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad of Iran opened Zimbabwe’s international trade fair in Bulawayo on Friday.

2010-04-25.title: Paul Schaefer, German Guilty of Chile Child Abuse, Dies at 89
2010-04-25.abstract: Mr. Schaefer was a former Nazi-era German soldier who founded a secretive German cult in southern Chile, where he sexually abused about 25 children.

2010-04-25.title: Ship Attack Shows South Korean Quandary Over How to Respond to North
2010-04-25.abstract: South Korea is inching closer to blaming North Korea for an attack, but the South’s options in responding appear to be limited.

2010-04-25.title: Thai Premier Rejects Protesters’ ‘Intimidation,’ but Seeks Peaceful End to Standoff
2010-04-25.abstract: In a televised address, Prime Minister Abhisit Vejjajiva rejected a call by the protesters known as the red shirts for a dissolution of Parliament within 30 days.

2010-04-25.title: 90,000 Protest U.S. Base on Okinawa
2010-04-25.abstract: In one of the largest protests on Okinawa, demonstrators rallied to oppose the relocation of an American air base.

2010-04-25.title: Israeli Rightists Stir Tensions in East Jerusalem
2010-04-25.abstract: A march by Israeli far-rightists through the volatile neighborhood raised passions over the city’s future.

2010-04-26.title: South Africa Redoubles Efforts Against AIDS
2010-04-26.abstract: The country is in the midst of a buildup of testing, treatment and prevention to expand its AIDS services.

2010-04-26.title: Socialists in Hungary Are Ousted in Elections
2010-04-26.abstract: The Fidesz party, which ran on a promise to restore “law and order” and pull Hungary from recession, won 263 of the 386 seats in Parliament.

2010-04-26.title: ‘Foolish’ Memo on Pope’s Visit Spurs Apology by the British
2010-04-26.abstract: An internal memo suggested, with apparent sarcasm, that an itinerary for Pope Benedict XVI might include opening an abortion clinic, blessing a gay marriage and introducing a “Benedict” brand of condoms.

2010-04-26.title: Indian Justice Inches Closer to Chapters of Violence
2010-04-26.abstract: Prosecutors are moving in on officials for fomenting sectarian violence, sparking hope that India might be ready to face its darkest moments.

2010-04-26.title: Investigation Links Critic’s Death to Top Chechens
2010-04-26.abstract: New evidence is raising questions about whether Chechnya’s president played a role in the killing of a whistleblower who was gunned down in 2009.

2010-04-27.title: Russia’s Power (and Strictly Platonic) Couple
2010-04-27.abstract: Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin mocked a reporter’s question about the secrets to success in a “political marriage,” saying he and President Dmitri A. Medvedev were just friends.

2010-04-27.title: Future Pope’s Role in Abuse Case Was Complex
2010-04-27.abstract: A case in the 1990s has become a kind of Rorschach test of the future Pope Benedict XVI’s treatment of sexual abuse.

2010-04-27.title: Noriega Extradited to France to Face Charges
2010-04-27.abstract: The former dictator of Panama will be tried on money laundering charges after his extradition from the United States.

2010-04-27.title: Pakistan's Power Crisis
2010-04-27.abstract: Adam Ellick reports from Lahore, Pakistan, about the worst power shortages the country has been facing in decades.

2010-04-27.title: Pakistanis Living on Brink, and Often in the Dark
2010-04-27.abstract: In response to an energy crisis, officials ordered power-saving measures that seemed to aggravate the problem and stifle some pleasures.

2010-04-28.title: A Little Danish Mermaid Comes Up for Air in China
2010-04-28.abstract: Denmark is moving its best-known national emblem, the bronze Little Mermaid statue, from the location it has occupied since 1913 to Shanghai for the 2010 World Expo.

2010-04-28.title: Report Details Torture at Secret Baghdad Prison
2010-04-28.abstract: The torture of Iraqi detainees at a secret prison in Baghdad was far more systematic and brutal than initially reported, Human Rights Watch reported.

2010-04-28.title: Obama Sticks to a Deadline in Iraq
2010-04-28.abstract: The post-election chaos in Iraq presents a test for President Obama’s vow to end the seven-year war.

2010-04-28.title: Despair Grows on Devastated Street in Haiti
2010-04-28.abstract: Now that the foreign doctors have left, many people on Avenue Poupelard feel they are on their own.

2010-04-28.title: On the Road With Gordon Brown
2010-04-28.abstract: As Prime Minister Gordon Brown finished a day of campaigning on Monday, the man who might not lead Britain for much longer dismissed the alarming reports about his prospects as merely agitation by the news media.

2010-04-29.title: For Migrants, New Law Is Just Another Challenge
2010-04-29.abstract: Arizona’s new immigration law is strict but many illegal immigrants endure  far worse on their way to the north.

2010-04-29.title: Merkel Tested as Escalating Greek Crisis Hurts Euro
2010-04-29.abstract: Political calculations by the German chancellor had to take a back seat to the more basic task of ensuring the stability of the euro.

2010-04-29.title:
In British Race, a Stray Remark
Haunts Brown
2010-04-29.abstract: Prime Minister Gordon Brown’s insult of a potential Labour voter, caught live on microphone, could cost his party votes.

2010-04-29.title: Tory Leader Treads Confidently on Labour’s Turf
2010-04-29.abstract: David Cameron’s relaxed manner suggests a sense that at least a narrow majority could be within the Conservative party’s grasp.

2010-04-29.title: Digging Deep and Seeing Greece’s Flaws
2010-04-29.abstract: Many Greeks are taking a hard look at their country these days and seeing mismanagement and corruption.

2010-04-30.title: In Abuse Crisis, a Church Is Pitted Against Society and Itself
2010-04-30.abstract: The Roman Catholic Church is undergoing nothing less than an epochal shift as the sexual abuse crisis continues to unfold.

2010-04-30.title: Iraq: Recount to Take Weeks
2010-04-30.abstract: The recount makes it unlikely that a new government will be seated by August, when the remaining American combat troops are scheduled to withdraw.

2010-04-30.title: Russia: Bombing Suspect Is Killed
2010-04-30.abstract: Russian authorities announced on Thursday that they had killed a militant who they said assisted in the March 29 suicide bombings in Moscow’s metro.

2010-04-30.title: Pakistan, in Shift, Weighs Attack on Militant Lair
2010-04-30.abstract: Western officials have long believed that North Waziristan is the single most important haven for militants with Al Qaeda and the Taliban.

2010-04-30.title: U.S. Report on Afghan War Finds Few Gains in 6 Months
2010-04-30.abstract: The report portrays an Afghan government with limited credibility among its people, an active insurgency and an enormous reliance on American troops for the foreseeable future.
